what_it_is: Accumulates particle attributes over time based on class and scope.
mental_model: Keeping a running total or history of particle data as frames advance.
example: Build up position offsets, counts, or custom attributes across frames.

Accumulate.Accumulate: Enables accumulation of attributes over time.
Accumulate.Attribute Class: Selects the attribute class to operate on (e.g. point, primitive).
Accumulate.Input Attribute Class Type: Defines how the incoming attribute class is interpreted.
Accumulate.Attribute Scope: Controls which attributes are included in the accumulation.

Common.Bypass: Disables the operator without removing it from the network.
Common.Free Extra GPU Memory: Releases unused GPU memory held by the operator.
Common.Parameter Color Space: Color space used for interpreting parameter values.
Common.Parameter Reference White: Reference white point for parameter color space.

explain_like_im_five: It remembers particle information from before and adds the new stuff on top.

operators.what_it_is: Imports geometry, animation, and attributes from Alembic files into POPs.
operators.mental_model: Loading a pre-baked 3D scene or animation into the particle system.
operators.example: Bring animated geometry from another DCC tool into a POP network.

Alembic.Object Path: Specifies which object inside the Alembic file to load.
Alembic.Transform: Applies transforms from the Alembic data.
Alembic.Time: Controls which time in the Alembic animation is sampled.
Alembic.Unload: Unloads the Alembic file from memory.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It brings a saved 3D animation into your particles.

operators.what_it_is: Computes statistical and structural information from particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Measuring and summarizing particle data to extract facts about it.
operators.example: Find the centroid of particles or the minimum and maximum values of an attribute.

Analyze.Analyze Attribute Class: Selects which attribute class is analyzed.
Analyze.Group: Limits analysis to a specific group.
Analyze.Number of Elements in Group: Counts elements in the selected group.
Analyze.Input Attributes: Attributes used as inputs for analysis.
Analyze.Append Attrib Names to Output: Adds attribute names to output results.
Analyze.Maximum Size: Finds the largest size or value.
Analyze.Index of Minimum: Index of the element with the minimum value.
Analyze.Index of Maximum: Index of the element with the maximum value.
Analyze.Verts Per Type: Outputs vertex counts per primitive type.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It looks at all the particles and tells you facts about them.

operators.what_it_is: Creates new attributes on particles.
operators.mental_model: Adding new data fields to particles.
operators.example: Create a custom float or color attribute for particles.

Create.Attribute Class: Selects the attribute class to create.
Create.Notification If Attribute Exists: Notifies if the attribute already exists.
Create.New Attribute: Enables creation of a new attribute.
Create.Name: Name of the new attribute.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It gives particles a new piece of information.

operators.what_it_is: Creates or modifies matrix-type attributes.
operators.mental_model: Attaching transformation tables to particles.
operators.example: Store custom transformation matrices per particle.

Matrix.Rows: Number of rows in the matrix.
Matrix.Column: Number of columns in the matrix.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It gives particles little math tables they can use.

operators.what_it_is: Renames existing particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Changing the label on particle data without changing the data itself.
operators.example: Rename an imported attribute to match your system.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes the name of particle information.

operators.what_it_is: Duplicates existing particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Copying particle data into a new attribute.
operators.example: Duplicate position data before modifying it.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a copy of particle information.

operators.what_it_is: Removes attributes from particles.
operators.mental_model: Stripping away data you no longer need.
operators.example: Delete temporary attributes after processing.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It throws away particle information you don’t want.

operators.what_it_is: Converts existing particle attributes into new attributes using defined operations.
operators.mental_model: Rewriting particle data into a different form or format.
operators.example: Convert a float attribute into a vector or remap values into a new attribute.

Attribute Convert.Convert Operations: Defines how input attributes are converted.
Attribute Convert.Delete Original: Removes the original attributes after conversion.
Attribute Convert.Notification If Attribute Exists: Notifies if the target attribute already exists.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes particle information into a new kind of information.

operators.what_it_is: Blends attributes from multiple POP inputs together.
operators.mental_model: Mixing particle data from different sources.
operators.example: Blend positions or colors from two particle streams.

Blend.Length Mismatch: How to handle differing attribute lengths.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It mixes particle information together.

operators.what_it_is: Maps particle attributes to operator parameters or targets.
operators.mental_model: Using particle data to drive other values.
operators.example: Map a particle attribute to control another operator’s parameter.

Mapping.Target: Specific parameter or target to drive.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets particle data control other things.

operators.what_it_is: Generates box geometry within POPs.
operators.mental_model: Creating simple box-shaped geometry for particles.
operators.example: Spawn particles as box shapes with adjustable size and orientation.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes little box shapes for particles.

operators.what_it_is: Stores particle states over time and allows indexed access to cached frames.
operators.mental_model: Recording particle history so you can look backward or step through time.
operators.example: Freeze particles at a previous frame or scrub through their motion.

Cache.Active Cache Size: Number of frames stored in the cache.
Cache.Step Size: Frame step interval between cached samples.
Cache.Output Index: Selects which cached frame is output.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It remembers what the particles did before so you can replay it.

operators.what_it_is: Blends particle data between cached frames using weights or channels.
operators.mental_model: Mixing different moments in particle history together.
operators.example: Smoothly blend between past particle states.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It mixes different remembered moments of particles.

operators.what_it_is: Selects specific frames from a particle cache.
operators.mental_model: Picking one moment from recorded particle history.
operators.example: Output only the particles from a specific cached frame.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It chooses one saved moment of the particles.

operators.what_it_is: Converts CHOP channel data into particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Turning timelines of numbers into particle information.
operators.example: Use audio or motion channels to drive particle attributes.

CHOP 2.Connectivity: Defines how channels map to points.
CHOP 2.Override Number of Points: Overrides point count based on channel data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns number tracks into particle information.

operators.what_it_is: Generates circular or arc-based geometry inside POPs.
operators.mental_model: Drawing rings or arcs that particles can follow or become.
operators.example: Create particles arranged in a circle or partial arc.

Circle.Arc Angles: Start and end angles for arcs.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes particles sit in a circle or arc.

operators.what_it_is: Controls point and vertex connectivity and ordering.
operators.mental_model: Deciding how points are linked and ordered.
operators.example: Reorder points or change how primitives are built.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides how points are connected together.

operators.what_it_is: Converts particle data between representations.
operators.mental_model: Changing how particle data is treated.
operators.example: Convert one particle form into another.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes how particle data is used.

operators.what_it_is: Creates multiple transformed copies of particles or geometry.
operators.mental_model: Duplicating particles and moving each copy.
operators.example: Make multiple rotated copies of a particle shape.

Copy.Copy ID Attribute: Stores copy index as an attribute.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes many copies and moves them around.

operators.what_it_is: Uses template data to drive transforms and orientation of particles.
operators.mental_model: Letting one set of data tell another how to move and rotate.
operators.example: Orient particles using normals from template points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets one set of particles tell another how to move.

operators.what_it_is: Runs a custom C++ POP plugin for particle processing.
operators.mental_model: Dropping compiled C++ code into the POP network as an operator.
operators.example: Use a custom POP plugin to implement a specialized simulation step.

C++.C++ Plugin Path: Path to the compiled C++ POP plugin.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you use your own C++ tool inside the particles.

operators.what_it_is: Generates or evaluates curve data for particles using curve parameters.
operators.mental_model: Making a controllable curve shape or curve-based values over a parameter.
operators.example: Generate a curve and sample along it to drive particle motion or attributes.

Curve.Curve Total Length: Total curve length used for parameterization.
Curve.Symmetric: Keeps curve behavior symmetric where applicable.
Curve.Start Value: Value at the start of the curve.
Curve.End Value: Value at the end of the curve.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a bendy line you can use to control things.

operators.what_it_is: Applies a lookup/remap to particle attributes using an index attribute and range mapping.
operators.mental_model: Using an attribute as a pointer into a remap table and converting values from one range to another.
operators.example: Map an index attribute to output values, then scale and offset the result.

Look Up.Lookup Index Attribute: Attribute used as the lookup index.
Look Up.Combine Operation: How lookup results combine with existing values.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses a number to pick a value, then remaps it how you want.

operators.what_it_is: Converts data from a DAT into particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Reading a table of values and turning its columns into particle data.
operators.example: Use a Points DAT to create per-particle attributes from spreadsheet-like data.

DAT 2.New Attribute: Creates a new attribute from DAT data.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It reads a table and gives the particles that information.

operators.what_it_is: Changes or restructures the dimensional layout of particle data.
operators.mental_model: Reorganizing particle data across dimensions.
operators.example: Reorder or collapse dimensions for downstream POP processing.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It rearranges how particle data is laid out.

operators.what_it_is: Defines and manages DMX fixtures driven by particle data.
operators.mental_model: Turning particles into lighting fixtures with addresses and channels.
operators.example: Drive multiple light fixtures using particle attributes.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns particles into controllable lights.

operators.what_it_is: Defines DMX channel profiles for fixtures.
operators.mental_model: Describing what each DMX channel does.
operators.example: Label DMX channels for intensity, color, or movement.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It names what each light channel means.

operators.what_it_is: Outputs DMX data from particles to lighting interfaces.
operators.mental_model: Sending particle-driven values out to real lights.
operators.example: Transmit particle values to a DMX lighting rig.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends particle values to real lights.

operators.what_it_is: Manages collections of DMX fixtures driven by POPs.
operators.mental_model: Grouping and controlling multiple fixtures at once.
operators.example: Control many lights using one particle system.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It manages groups of lights together.

operators.what_it_is: Outputs DMX or control data over a serial connection.
operators.mental_model: Sending particle data through a physical cable.
operators.example: Send DMX data to a serial-based lighting device.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends particle data through a cable.

operators.what_it_is: Configures network-based DMX output protocols.
operators.mental_model: Sending lighting data over the network.
operators.example: Output Art-Net or KiNet data driven by particles.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends lighting data over the network.

operators.what_it_is: Applies per-channel multipliers to DMX output.
operators.mental_model: Scaling light values before they are sent.
operators.example: Dim specific DMX channels using multipliers.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It scales how strong each light value is.

operators.what_it_is: Extrudes particle geometry along a direction or distance.
operators.mental_model: Pulling faces outward to give particles thickness or depth.
operators.example: Extrude particle surfaces to create volume.

Extrude.Use Distance Attribute: Uses an attribute to control extrusion distance.
Extrude.Copy Topology Info Back to CPU: Copies topology data from GPU back to CPU.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It pulls shapes outward to make them stick out.

operators.what_it_is: Modifies geometry by splitting, smoothing, or cleaning facets.
operators.mental_model: Adjusting how faces and points are organized and simplified.
operators.example: Remove unused points or refine geometry structure.

Facet.Distance: Distance threshold for merging or splitting.
Facet.Remove Unused Points: Deletes points not used by primitives.
Facet.Copy Topology Info Back to CPU: Copies topology data from GPU back to CPU.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It fixes and cleans up the shape.

operators.what_it_is: Feeds particle data back into itself over time.
operators.mental_model: Letting particles remember and reuse their past state.
operators.example: Create iterative or accumulating particle behavior.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets particles use what they did before.

operators.what_it_is: Generates spatial fields that influence particles using distance, range, and directional controls.
operators.mental_model: Invisible forces in space that push, pull, or weight particles.
operators.example: Apply a falloff force between two points that affects nearby particles.

Field.Attribute Class: Attribute class the field operates on.
Field.Field Attribute Scopes: Scopes for attributes affected by the field.

Transition.Transition Range: Range over which the transition occurs.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes invisible forces in space that affect particles.

operators.what_it_is: Loads geometry data from files into POPs.
operators.mental_model: Bringing saved shapes into the particle system.
operators.example: Import geometry from a file to use as particle sources.

File In.Input Reference White: Reference white for input color space.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It loads shapes from a file.

operators.what_it_is: Writes particle geometry and attributes to disk.
operators.mental_model: Recording particle results to files.
operators.example: Export particle geometry per frame to disk.

File Out.Unique Suffix: Adds a unique suffix to filenames.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It saves what the particles look like to a file.

operators.what_it_is: Applies directional forces to particles using configurable falloff and modes.
operators.mental_model: Pushing or pulling particles through space with adjustable force shapes.
operators.example: Apply a radial force with falloff to attract particles toward a point.

Force.Position Direction: Direction or position reference for the force.
Force.Falloff Radius: Radius over which the force falls off.
Force.Limit Near Range: Limits force close to the source.

Global.Global Force: Applies a global force to all particles.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It pushes particles around in different ways, like wind or spinning.

operators.what_it_is: Runs a GPU compute shader to read and write particle attributes in parallel.
operators.mental_model: A programmable GPU worker that processes particle data in massive parallel batches.
operators.example: Use a compute shader to update positions, velocities, or custom attributes with custom GLSL logic.

GLSL Compute Shader.Attribute Class: Selects which attribute class is processed (Point, Primitive, or Vertex).
GLSL Compute Shader.Number of Threads: Threading mode (Auto, Number of Elements and Other Input, Manual Number of Elements, Number of Elements From Attribute and Manual).
GLSL Compute Shader.Threads Input: Input source used to derive thread counts.
GLSL Compute Shader.POP: POP used as an input reference for element counts.
GLSL Compute Shader.Num Elements Attribute: Attribute that defines number of elements.
GLSL Compute Shader.Copy Previous Pass: Copies results from the previous pass.
GLSL Compute Shader.Output to Input: Feeds shader output back as input.
GLSL Compute Shader.TD Simplex Noise: Enables TD simplex noise helpers for shader use.

Create Attributes.New Attribute Name: Name of the attribute to create.
Create Attributes.Custom and Color: Creates custom attributes and color attributes.
Create Attributes.Text: Text used for attribute creation or labeling.
Create Attributes.Point Scale: Point scale setting for generated attributes.
Create Attributes.Line Width: Line width setting for generated attributes.

Samplers.TOP: TOP used as the sampler source.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets the GPU run your math on particle data really fast.

operators.what_it_is: Runs custom GPU code that can read and change particle data.
operators.mental_model: A very fast worker that runs the same instructions on lots of particles at once.
operators.example: Use GPU code to move particles, change their color, or make new attributes.

GLSL Compute Shader.Attribute Class: Which kind of data is being worked on (point, primitive, or vertex).
GLSL Compute Shader.Number of Threads: How many GPU workers are used (automatic or manually set).
GLSL Compute Shader.Threads Input: Where the thread count comes from.
GLSL Compute Shader.POP: POP used to decide how many items there are.
GLSL Compute Shader.Num Elements Attribute: Attribute that tells how many items to process.
GLSL Compute Shader.Workgroup Size: How GPU workers are grouped together.
GLSL Compute Shader.Dispatch Size: How many worker groups are launched.
GLSL Compute Shader.Output Attributes: Which particle attributes get written to.
GLSL Compute Shader.Output Access: Whether the shader only writes or can also read and write.
GLSL Compute Shader.Copy Previous Pass: Starts with results from the last pass.
GLSL Compute Shader.Output to Input: Feeds the result back into the next pass.
GLSL Compute Shader.Passes: How many times the shader runs.
GLSL Compute Shader.TD Simplex Noise: Gives the shader built-in noise tools.

Create Attributes.Text: Text used for labels or setup.

Samplers.TOP: Image or texture used by the shader.

Arrays.Type: What the array holds (numbers or vectors).
Arrays.CHOP and Uniform: Where the array data comes from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets the GPU do fast math on particles all at once.

operators.what_it_is: Runs advanced GPU shader code that can create and control particle geometry and attributes.
operators.mental_model: A powerful GPU machine where you decide exactly how many workers run, what they touch, and what geometry comes out.
operators.example: Use GPU code to generate new points, lines, or primitives and fully control their attributes.

GLSL Advanced.Compute Shader: The shader code that runs on the GPU.
GLSL Advanced.Shader Dispatch Mode: How the shader is launched (single dispatch or per-primitive batch).
GLSL Advanced.Number of Threads Per Input: How many GPU workers are used per input item.
GLSL Advanced.Manual Number of Elements: Manually sets how many items are processed.
GLSL Advanced.Number of Elements From Attribute: Uses an attribute to decide element count.
GLSL Advanced.Manual Number of Threads: Manually sets how many GPU threads run.
GLSL Advanced.Number of Elements: Total number of items to process.
GLSL Advanced.Number of Elements Attribute: Attribute that defines how many elements exist.
GLSL Advanced.Output Access: Controls whether data is written or read and written.
GLSL Advanced.Copy Previous Pass Output to Input: Uses last pass results as input.
GLSL Advanced.Passes: How many times the shader runs.
GLSL Advanced.TD Simplex Noise: Built-in noise tools for the shader.

Output.Max Points From Input or Custom: Uses input limits or custom limits.

Extra Outputs.Extra Output Name POP: POP used to name extra outputs

menu.Colors: Same as GLSL Compute Shader colors setup.
menu.Vectors: Same as GLSL Compute Shader vectors setup.
menu.Samplers: Same as GLSL Compute Shader samplers setup.
menu.Arrays: Same as GLSL Compute Shader arrays setup.
menu.Matrices: Same as GLSL Compute Shader matrices setup.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you use the GPU to build and control particles and shapes exactly how you want.

operators.what_it_is: Uses GPU code to make many copies of particles or geometry.
operators.mental_model: A fast copy machine on the GPU that duplicates things and can change each copy.
operators.example: Create many GPU-driven copies of points and modify each one differently.

GLSL Copy.Point Compute Shader: GPU code that runs for each point copy.
GLSL Copy.Point Compute Attributes: Attributes used by the point shader.
GLSL Copy.Verts Compute Method: How vertex data is handled on the GPU.
GLSL Copy.Prim Compute Method: How primitive data is handled on the GPU.
GLSL Copy.Append Dimension: Adds an extra dimension for copies.
GLSL Copy.TD Simplex Noise: Built-in noise tools for GPU code.

Create Attributes.New Attribute: Turns on creating a new attribute.
Create Attributes.New Attribute Class: Where the attribute lives (point, primitive, or vertex).

menu.Colors: Controls color attributes used by the GPU.
menu.Vectors: Controls vector attributes used by the GPU.
menu.Samplers: Images or textures used by the GPU.
menu.Arrays: Lists of values used by the GPU.
menu.Matrices: Matrix values used by the GPU.

POP Buffers.Attribute Class: Type of data (point, primitive, or vertex).
POP Buffers.Name: Name used in the shader.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes lots of copies really fast using the GPU.

operators.what_it_is: Selects data coming out of a GLSL operator and names it.
operators.mental_model: Picking one GPU result and giving it a label.
operators.example: Choose a specific GLSL output to use later in the network.

GLSL Select.GLSL Select POP: Which GLSL POP to pull data from.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks one GPU result and names it.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a grid of points or primitives.
operators.mental_model: Laying down a flat checkerboard made of particles.
operators.example: Make a grid of points to use as a base shape.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat grid of points.

operators.what_it_is: Creates and edits groups of points, vertices, or primitives.
operators.mental_model: Tagging particles so you can work on only some of them.
operators.example: Put certain points into a group for later use.

Create.Entity: What kind of thing is grouped.
Create.Attribute: Attribute used to define the group.
Create.Combine: How this group combines with others.

Thin.Combine: How thinning combines with other rules.

Edit.Primitive to Point Group: Converts prim group to point group.
Edit.Point to Primitive Group: Converts point group to prim group.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you pick which particles belong together.

operators.what_it_is: Counts how many values fall into ranges.
operators.mental_model: Sorting numbers into buckets and counting how many land in each one.
operators.example: Count how many particles fall into different value ranges.

Histogram.Attribute Class: Which data type is used (point, primitive, or vertex).
Histogram.Outside Range: How values outside the range are handled.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It counts how many things fall into each number bucket.

operators.what_it_is: Pulls geometry and animation from another operator.
operators.mental_model: Choosing what to bring in from another place.
operators.example: Import animated geometry from a parent operator.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It brings shapes and animation in from somewhere else.

operators.what_it_is: Defines an input connection.
operators.mental_model: A labeled plug where data comes in.
operators.example: Name and order inputs coming into a POP network.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It names where the data comes in.

operators.what_it_is: Clamps values so they stay within limits.
operators.mental_model: Putting guard rails on numbers so they don’t go too far.
operators.example: Keep an attribute between a minimum and maximum value.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It stops numbers from going too high or too low.

operators.what_it_is: Snaps values to fixed steps.
operators.mental_model: Rounding numbers to even steps.
operators.example: Snap particle values to a grid of steps.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It rounds numbers to neat steps.

operators.what_it_is: Builds lines from points and controls how smooth or detailed they are.
operators.mental_model: Connecting dots to make lines, then deciding how smooth and detailed those lines become.
operators.example: Turn moving points into smooth trails or paths.

Setup.Subdivided Lines: Uses subdivided lines instead of straight segments.
Setup.Control Points: Uses control points to shape the line.
Setup.Multiple Line Strips: Creates more than one line strip.
Setup.Tension: How tight or loose the curve is.
Setup.Enable Weights: Uses weights to affect curve shape.
Setup.Closed: Closes the line into a loop.

Output.New Attribute: Creates a new attribute on the line.
Output.Name: Name of the new attribute.

Points.Point Position: Uses point positions to build lines.

Division.Division Method: How points are added along the line.
Division.Divisions Per Segment: Number of divisions for each segment.
Division.Divisions Defined Per Segment: Each segment controls its own divisions.
Division.Add Control Point Attribute: Adds an attribute to control points.
Division.Independent Variable Attribute Maximum N: Limits how many points can be created.
Division.Max Times for Binary Steps: Limits how many refinement steps are used.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It connects dots to make lines and smooths them how you want.

operators.what_it_is: Breaks lines into separate pieces based on rules.
operators.mental_model: Deciding where a long line should snap into smaller lines.
operators.example: Split a trail into separate line strips when points get too far apart.

Line Break.One Line Per Strip: Each strip becomes its own line.
Line Break.Line Break Attribute: Attribute used to decide where to break.
Line Break.Line Strip Index Attribute: Attribute that defines which strip a point belongs to.
Line Break.End by Distance Threshold: Breaks when points are too far apart.
Line Break.Copy Topology Info Back to CPU: Sends line structure info back to the CPU.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts long lines into smaller lines when rules say so.

operators.what_it_is: Adds more points along lines to make them smoother or more detailed.
operators.mental_model: Filling in extra dots along a line.
operators.example: Add evenly spaced points along a line strip.

Divide.Distance by Curvature: Adds more points where the line bends.
Divide.Maximum Number of Vertices: Limits how many points can be created.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds more dots along a line so it looks smoother.

operators.what_it_is: Measures distances, directions, and shape changes along points or vertices.
operators.mental_model: Looking at each point and asking how far and which way its neighbors are.
operators.example: Find how sharp a line bends or how far each point is from the next one.

Metrics.Attribute Class: Which data type is measured (point or vertex).
Metrics.Displacement to Next: Vector from this point to the next one.
Metrics.Displacement to Previous: Vector from this point to the previous one.
Metrics.Direction to Next: Direction pointing to the next point.
Metrics.Direction to Previous: Direction pointing to the previous point.
Metrics.Tangent: Direction of the line at the point.
Metrics.Max Neighbor: Limits how far neighbors are checked.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It measures how far points are and which way the line bends.

operators.what_it_is: Computes distances and lengths along line strips.
operators.mental_model: Measuring how far along a line each point is.
operators.example: Give each point a value from start to end of the line.

Line Strip.Distance from Start: Distance from the beginning of the line.
Line Strip.Distance from End: Distance from the end of the line.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells you how far along the line each point is.

operators.what_it_is: Gives points their position numbers inside line strips.
operators.mental_model: Numbering the points along a line.
operators.example: Know which point comes first, second, or last.

Index.Vert Index in Line Strip: Index of the point in the line.
Index.Number of Verts in Line Strip: How many points are in the line.
Index.Line Strip Index: Which line strip the point belongs to.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It numbers the dots along each line.

operators.what_it_is: Rebuilds lines with evenly spaced or rule-based points.
operators.mental_model: Redrawing a line so the dots are spaced the way you want.
operators.example: Make points evenly spaced along a curve.

Line Resample.Resample Method: How points are added or spaced.
Line Resample.Divisions Per Line Strip: Adds a fixed number of points.
Line Resample.Distance Between Points by Curvature: Adds more points where the line bends.
Line Resample.Min Max Bias: Bias between min and max spacing.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It redraws the line so the dots are spaced better.

operators.what_it_is: Smooths lines so they bend more gently and evenly.
operators.mental_model: Rubbing out sharp corners so the line feels softer.
operators.example: Smooth noisy line strips so motion looks more fluid.

Line Smooth.Attribute Class: Which data is smoothed (point, primitive, or vertex).
Line Smooth.Input Attribute Class: Which input data type is read.
Line Smooth.Max Distance: Limits how far points can move.
Line Smooth.Max Number of Vertices: Caps how many points the line can have.
Line Smooth.Smooth by Edge Distance: Smooths based on distance between edges.
Line Smooth.Fixed End: Locks the start or end of the line.
Line Smooth.Output Attribute Scope: Where the smoothed result is written.

Resample.Distance Between Points by Curvature: Spaces points based on how much the line bends.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes wiggly lines smoother and nicer.

operators.what_it_is: Uses values to drive other parameters.
operators.mental_model: Turning one number into control for something else.
operators.example: Use a line value to control force strength.

Map.Combine Operations: How the new value is applied.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets one value control another thing.

operators.what_it_is: Uses one attribute to look up values from another attribute.
operators.mental_model: Using a number as a pointer to grab another value.
operators.example: Use an index attribute to pick values from a list of attributes.

Look Up Attribute.Attribute Class: Which data is used (point, primitive, or vertex).
Look Up Attribute.Group: Limits the lookup to a group.
Look Up Attribute.Normalize or Point Index: Treats index as normalized or as a point index.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses one number to pick another value.

operators.what_it_is: Uses a CHOP channel to look up values for particles.
operators.mental_model: Reading values from a timeline using an index.
operators.example: Use animation channels to drive particle attributes.

Look Up Channel.Group: Limits the lookup to a group.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses a timeline to give particles values.

operators.what_it_is: Looks up values from an image or texture.
operators.mental_model: Reading colors from an image using coordinates.
operators.example: Sample a texture to color particles.

Look Up Texture.Input Extend Mode: How edges of the texture behave.
Look Up Texture.Output Attribute Scope: Where the sampled result is written.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It reads colors from a picture and gives them to particles.

operators.what_it_is: Does basic math on particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Taking numbers on particles and adjusting them with math.
operators.example: Scale, offset, remap, or round an attribute value.

Math.Attribute Class: Which data is changed (point, primitive, or vertex).
Math.Pre-Add: Adds a value before other math.
Math.Post-Add: Adds a value after other math.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes numbers on particles using math.

operators.what_it_is: Combines values from multiple inputs and runs math on them.
operators.mental_model: Taking a few numbers, mixing them together, then adjusting the result.
operators.example: Add two attributes together, then remap and round the result.

Math Combine.Result Scope: Where the combined result is written.

Combine.Operation: How values are combined (add, subtract, multiply, etc.).

Output.Output: Final output settings for the result.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It mixes numbers together and fixes them up before sending them out.

operators.what_it_is: Controls how multiple inputs are read and matched together.
operators.mental_model: Deciding how different input streams line up when doing math or combining data.
operators.example: Tell the system what to do when one input has more points than another.

Inputs.Attribute Class: Which data type is used (point, primitive, or vertex).
Inputs.Length Mismatch: What happens when inputs don’t match in size.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides how different inputs line up and mix together.

operators.what_it_is: Joins groups or geometry from multiple inputs.
operators.mental_model: Putting piles of particles together into one pile.
operators.example: Merge points from several POPs into one stream.

Merge.Points or Primitives: Choose what kind of data to merge.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts multiple particle groups together.

operators.what_it_is: Finds nearby points and measures relationships between them.
operators.mental_model: Looking around each point to see who its neighbors are.
operators.example: Find the closest points and average their values.

Neighbor.Add Prefix: Adds a prefix to output names.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It looks at nearby points and uses their values.

operators.what_it_is: Adds noise values to particle data.
operators.mental_model: Filling space with controlled randomness and giving particles values from it.
operators.example: Use noise to wiggle positions, vary colors, or add organic motion.

Noise.Noise Lookup Attribute: Attribute used to sample the noise.
Noise.Period: Repeats the noise over a fixed range.
Noise.Attribute Class: Where noise is applied (point, primitive, or vertex).

Transform.Transform: Moves or rotates the noise space.

Output.Noise Gradient: Outputs the slope of the noise.
Output.Combine Operation: How noise mixes with existing values.
Output.Combine Entity: What kind of data is combined.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds controlled randomness so things look less stiff.

operators.what_it_is: Scales values so they fit nicely into a range.
operators.mental_model: Squishing numbers so they fit between two limits.
operators.example: Normalize an attribute so it stays between 0 and 1.

Normalize.Attribute Class: Which data is changed (point, primitive, or vertex).
Normalize.Replace Errors: Fixes bad values like NaN or infinity.
Normalize.Lookup Curve: Uses a curve to shape the result.
Normalize.Exponent: Changes how fast values rise or fall.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It squishes numbers so they fit between two ends.

operators.what_it_is: Does nothing except pass data through.
operators.mental_model: A placeholder or pause point in the network.
operators.example: Use it to organize or temporarily disable part of a network.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It passes things through without changing them.

operators.what_it_is: Selects data from an OAK camera or device.
operators.mental_model: Choosing which camera stream to use.
operators.example: Pull depth or image data from an OAK device.

OAK Select.CHOP: CHOP used for control or sync.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks data from a camera.

operators.what_it_is: Marks the output of a POP network.
operators.mental_model: The exit door for particle data.
operators.example: Send particles out of a network to be used elsewhere.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It sends the particles out.

operators.what_it_is: Creates and simulates particles over time.
operators.mental_model: A particle factory that makes, moves, and ages particles.
operators.example: Emit particles that move, slow down, and die.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes particles that move around and disappear.

operators.what_it_is: Creates repeating patterns along points or lines.
operators.mental_model: Stamping a rhythm or pattern over points or along a line.
operators.example: Make a repeating wave or on/off pattern along a line strip.

Pattern.Number of Points: How many points the pattern uses.
Pattern.Cyclic: Repeats the pattern in a loop.
Pattern.Number of Cycles: How many times the pattern repeats.
Pattern.Line Breaking Cycle: Breaks lines based on the pattern.
Pattern.Closed: Closes the pattern into a loop.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes repeating shapes or rhythms along points.

operators.what_it_is: Offsets values along a range, like sliding a phase over data.
operators.mental_model: Sliding values forward or backward along a line.
operators.example: Offset an effect so it travels along points over time.

Phaser.Attribute Class: Which data is changed (point, primitive, or vertex).
Phaser.Phase: How much the values are shifted.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It slides values along so effects move smoothly.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a flat plane made of points or primitives.
operators.mental_model: Making a flat sheet like graph paper.
operators.example: Create a flat surface for particles to sit on.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat sheet of points.

operators.what_it_is: Creates points and basic point attributes.
operators.mental_model: Making dots and giving them names and positions.
operators.example: Create points and store their positions in an attribute.

Setup.Create Point Primitives: Creates point primitives instead of loose points.
Setup.Name: Name of the new attribute.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes dots and remembers where they are.

operators.what_it_is: Loads points and their attributes from a file.
operators.mental_model: Reading dots from a saved file.
operators.example: Load point positions and colors from disk.

Point File In.Max Number of Points: Limits how many points are loaded.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It loads dots from a file.

operators.what_it_is: Removes points based on a pattern or spacing.
operators.mental_model: Keeping some dots and throwing others away.
operators.example: Thin points so there are fewer of them.

Thin.Thin Range Length: How wide the thinning range is.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It removes some dots so there aren’t too many.

operators.what_it_is: Remaps values from one range to another.
operators.mental_model: Stretching or squishing numbers into a new range.
operators.example: Remap values from 0–100 into 0–1.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It moves numbers from one scale to another.

operators.what_it_is: Creates points inside simple shapes.
operators.mental_model: Sprinkling dots inside a shape.
operators.example: Generate random points inside a sphere or along a line.

Point Generator.Shape: Which shape points are generated in.

Transform.Transform: Moves, rotates, and scales the shape.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes dots inside shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Turns image data into geometry.
operators.mental_model: Tracing shapes out of an image.
operators.example: Create geometry from a black-and-white image.

Polygonize.Inside Threshold: What counts as inside the shape.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns pictures into shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Creates or edits primitives made from points.
operators.mental_model: Deciding how dots are connected into shapes.
operators.example: Build lines or faces from points.

Post.Copy Topology Info Back to CPU: Sends structure info back to CPU.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It connects dots into shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Projects points from one space into another.
operators.mental_model: Flattening or mapping points using a camera view.
operators.example: Project 3D points into screen space.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It maps points through a camera view.

operators.what_it_is: Creates connections or values based on how close points are to each other.
operators.mental_model: Looking around each point to see which other points are nearby.
operators.example: Draw lines between points that are close together.

Proximity.Max Lines Per Point: Limits how many connections one point can make.

Attributes.Attribute Class: Which data is affected (point, primitive, or vertex).

operators.explain_like_im_five: It connects points that are close to each other.

operators.what_it_is: Generates random values or extra points.
operators.mental_model: Rolling dice to get new numbers or points.
operators.example: Add random offsets or spawn extra random points.

Random.Extra Points Per Source Point: Adds more points per input point.
Random.CHOP: CHOP used as a random source.

Combine.Combined Operations: How random values mix with existing ones.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds randomness so things don’t look the same.

operators.what_it_is: Shoots rays into geometry and reports what they hit.
operators.mental_model: Throwing invisible sticks into the world and seeing what they touch.
operators.example: Detect where particles hit a surface and get the hit position and normal.

Ray.Number of Bounces: How many times the ray can bounce.
Ray.Limit Ray Length: Caps how far rays can travel.
Ray.Hardware Ray Tracing: Uses GPU ray tracing if available.
Ray.Hit Normal: Outputs the surface direction at the hit.
Ray.Farthest Hit: Uses the farthest hit instead of nearest.
Ray.Lift Hit Points: Moves hit points off the surface.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shoots invisible lines and tells you what they hit.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a flat rectangular shape.
operators.mental_model: Making a flat card or screen in space.
operators.example: Create a flat surface for projection or particles.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat rectangle.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders or remaps attribute values.
operators.mental_model: Shuffling numbers into a new order.
operators.example: Reorder attribute values across points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It rearranges numbers into a new order.

operators.what_it_is: Splits activity or motion into parts and outputs related surface data.
operators.mental_model: Breaking movement into pieces and labeling how each piece behaves.
operators.example: Divide motion and output normals or texture coordinates.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It breaks motion into parts and tells you which way things face.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses which attributes are passed through.
operators.mental_model: Picking which information is allowed to continue.
operators.example: Only pass point attributes and ignore the rest.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides what information gets through.

operators.what_it_is: Connects points into a surface, like wrapping skin over a shape.
operators.mental_model: Stretching fabric over a frame.
operators.example: Create a surface between multiple curves.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It stretches a surface over points.

operators.what_it_is: Deforms points using a skeleton or bones.
operators.mental_model: Bending a mesh using a puppet skeleton.
operators.example: Animate points using bone movement.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It bends points using bones.

operators.what_it_is: Brings geometry from SOPs into a POP network.
operators.mental_model: Taking shapes made somewhere else and handing them to particles.
operators.example: Use a SOP mesh as input data for a particle system.

SOP to.Input Reference White: Reference white used for color conversion.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It takes shapes from somewhere else and gives them to particles.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders points based on a rule.
operators.mental_model: Lining dots up in a specific order.
operators.example: Sort points by height, distance, or a custom value.

Point.Attribute in Uint: Treats the attribute as whole numbers.
Point.Shift: Shifts the order forward or backward.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts the dots in a new order.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders primitives based on a rule.
operators.mental_model: Lining shapes up in a specific order.
operators.example: Sort faces or lines by size or position.

Primitive.Attribute in Uint: Treats the attribute as whole numbers.
Primitive.Shift: Shifts the order forward or backward.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts shapes in a new order.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a spherical shape made of points or lines.
operators.mental_model: Making a ball out of dots.
operators.example: Generate a sphere of points to emit particles from.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a ball out of dots.

operators.what_it_is: Randomly places points on or inside geometry.
operators.mental_model: Sprinkling dots like salt.
operators.example: Scatter points across a surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It scatters dots around.

operators.what_it_is: Adds more detail by splitting geometry.
operators.mental_model: Cutting shapes into smaller pieces.
operators.example: Smooth geometry by adding more points.

Subdivide.Copy Topology Info Back to CPU: Sends structure info to the CPU.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts shapes into smaller pieces.

operators.what_it_is: Chooses between multiple inputs.
operators.mental_model: A fork in the road where you pick one path.
operators.example: Switch between different particle sources.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks which input to use.

operators.what_it_is: Applies image textures to geometry using coordinates or projection.
operators.mental_model: Wrapping a picture onto shapes.
operators.example: Project a texture onto points or primitives.

Texture.Projection Axis: Axis used for projecting the texture.
Texture.Apply To: What data type gets the texture.

Transform.Transform: Moves, rotates, or scales the texture space.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sticks a picture onto shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Turns image data into particle attributes.
operators.mental_model: Reading pixels from a picture and giving them to points.
operators.example: Use image brightness to control particle size or color.

Inputs.New Attribute: Creates an attribute from the image.

Depth.Delete Near Points: Removes points too close to camera.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns picture pixels into numbers for dots.

operators.what_it_is: Controls how points are turned into renderable geometry.
operators.mental_model: Deciding how dots become lines, faces, or strips.
operators.example: Tell the system how to build triangles or lines from points.

Topology.Point Index Attribute Class: Which attribute class stores point indices.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides how dots turn into shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a donut-shaped form.
operators.mental_model: Making a ring out of dots.
operators.example: Generate a torus of points for effects or emission.

Torus.Columns: Number of divisions around the ring.
Torus.Rows: Number of divisions across the tube.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a donut shape.

operators.what_it_is: Builds trails from moving points over time.
operators.mental_model: Leaving a breadcrumb trail behind moving dots.
operators.example: Show the path particles have traveled.

Trail.Increment: How often new points are added.
Trail.Oldest Point First: Orders trail points from oldest to newest.

Transform.Transform: Moves, rotates, or scales the trails.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows where dots have been.

operators.what_it_is: Moves, rotates, or scales geometry or attributes.
operators.mental_model: Picking something up and moving or turning it.
operators.example: Move points upward and rotate them over time.

Transform.Attribute Class: What kind of data is affected.
Transform.Weight Attribute: Controls how strong the transform is.
Transform.Xform Matrix CHOP DAT: Uses a matrix from CHOP or DAT.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It moves and turns things.

operators.what_it_is: Applies trigonometry math to attributes.
operators.mental_model: Using sine and cosine to make waves.
operators.example: Turn a value into a smooth oscillation.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes smooth math waves.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a tube or cylinder shape.
operators.mental_model: Making a pipe out of dots.
operators.example: Generate a vertical tube for particles.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a pipe shape.

operators.what_it_is: Twists geometry around an axis.
operators.mental_model: Twisting a towel.
operators.example: Add a spiral twist to points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It twists things like a towel.

operators.what_it_is: Uses ZED camera depth and image data.
operators.mental_model: Turning what a depth camera sees into points.
operators.example: Create point clouds from a ZED camera.

ZED.Output Mode: What kind of data is output.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns a camera view into dots.
